title: Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
description: In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving

Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving

allStepDetails.content:
Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving
Step Name:Step 1 - Importing Data
Detailed Instructions:
Connecticut Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021 Step By Step

Download the 'Connecticut Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021'  file by clicking the red icon above.
Open Power BI Desktop
Click on Get data button 
Select Text/CSV and click on connect 
Select the 'Connecticut Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021' CSV file and click on Open
A preview of the data will be displayed, click on Load

Insight: 

Data Link :-
https://drive.google.com/drive/folders/1DADCVbroQ1okQfF0Sv3PGZovAoVelpS3?usp=share_link

Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving
Step Name:STEP 2 Prepare Database
Detailed Instructions:

1. Add index column for (accidental _drugs deaths) by click on add column from robin and click on index column chose it start from number one as show below  .

2. Merge city and county columns as GIF below, click on add column ,column by example , type just first row and press entre then click on OK.

3.Right click on merge column and chose last option add as new query ,remove all duplicate ,then convert it as table add index for and rename table as county  and column as map.

4. for adding pictures for each county you can duplicate map column and search every each county in google copy link photo and replace county duplicate name with photo link  follow me as i do it in gif below.

5 .Merge county table with (accidental _drugs deaths )  Table as GIF below , from home chose merge queries ,then you can delete all merge column , death state ,county , city.

6.Right click on Sex column and chose last option add as new query ,remove all duplicate ,then convert it as table add index for and rename table as Sex  and column as Sex.

7.Merge Sex table with (MainT )  Table as GIF below , from home chose merge queries ,then you can delete Sex Column.

8.Right click on Race column and chose last option add as new query ,remove all duplicate ,then convert it as table then add index for table and rename table as Race and column as Race .

9.Merge Race table with (MainT)  Table as GIF below , from home chose merge queries ,then you can delete Race Column.

10 .Right click on Location column and chose last option add as new query ,remove all duplicate ,then convert it as table add index for location table and rename table as Place and column as Place .

11.Merge Place table with (MainT)  Table as GIF below , from home chose merge queries ,then you can delete location Column.

12.Now one table left is for Data Refreshed Date by click on Data source  then click on advance query you can use M language copy and past.

let
    Source = DateTime.LocalNow(),
    #"Converted to Table" = #table(1, {{Source}}),
    #"Renamed Columns" = Table.RenameColumns(#"Converted to Table",{{"Column1", "DateTime"}}),
    #"Duplicated Column" = Table.DuplicateColumn(#"Renamed Columns", "DateTime", "DateTime - Copy"),
    #"Duplicated Column1" = Table.DuplicateColumn(#"Duplicated Column", "DateTime", "DateTime - Copy.1"),
    #"Renamed Columns1" = Table.RenameColumns(#"Duplicated Column1",{{"DateTime - Copy", "Date"}, {"DateTime - Copy.1", "Time"}}),
    #"Extracted Date" = Table.TransformColumns(#"Renamed Columns1",{{"Date", DateTime.Date, type date}}),
    #"Extracted Time" = Table.TransformColumns(#"Extracted Date",{{"Time", DateTime.Time, type time}})
in
    #"Extracted Time"

13. Create column in mainT table age Range 
Age Rang = IF('MainT'[Age]<20,"0-20")&
            IF(AND('MainT'[Age]>=20,'MainT'[Age]<30),"20-30") &
            IF(AND('MainT'[Age]>=30,'MainT'[Age]<40),"30-40")&
            IF(AND('MainT'[Age]>=40,'MainT'[Age]<50),"40-50") &
            IF(AND('MainT'[Age]>=50,'MainT'[Age]<60),"50-60") &
            IF(AND('MainT'[Age]>=60,'MainT'[Age]<90),"60-90")

Insight: 

Note :- I can use table as is it but is good for make data size from 3 Mb to 1Mb at seem time good practice to use data such as sql data and make it faster than as is it .

Note :- you can use it for any project that's (M Language) above for Date &time  Refresh.

Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving
Step Name:Step 3 Dax Calculation
Detailed Instructions:
Step 3 Dax Calculation

1.Create table and rename it as Measures  for all calculations .

Adding Measures 
adding measures ,copy measure and then right click on measure table select from list new measure past follow me as how i do it in Gif below.

TotalD = COUNTA(MainT[ID])
Total Sum =  CALCULATE(COUNT(MainT[ID]),DATESYTD('Calendar'[Date],"31/12"))
MoM% = 
                Var _LM = CALCULATE([TotalD],
                DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,MONTH))
Return 
                DIVIDE([TotalD]-_lm,_lm)
Arrow M = SWITCH(TRUE(),
                                       [MoM%]>=0 , UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))
Color M = IF([Arrow M]=UNICHAR(9650), "Red" , "Green")
YoY% =
                VAR _LYA = CALCULATE([Total Sum],
                DATEADD('Calendar'[Date],-1,YEAR))
Return
                DIVIDE([Total Sum]-_LYA,_LYA)
Arrow Y = SWITCH(TRUE(),
                            [YoY%]>=0 , UNICHAR(9650), UNICHAR(9660))
Color Y = IF([Arrow Y]=UNICHAR(9650), "Red" , "Green")

AVGFemale = AVERAGEX(Values('Calendar'[Year]),
                       CALCULATE([TotalD],USERELATIONSHIP(Sex[IDS],MainT[Sex.1.IDS]),
                       Sex,Sex[Sex]="FEMALE"))
                 
AVGMale = 
                       AVERAGEX(Values('Calendar'[Year]),
                       CALCULATE([TotalD],USERELATIONSHIP(MainT[Sex.1.IDS],Sex[IDS]),
                       Sex[Sex]="male"))
AVG AGE = VAR _totalage =Sum('MainT'[Age])
                    Var _TotalRows = COUNTROWS('MainT')
Return 
                    DIVIDE(_totalage,_TotalRows)

3. Create Calendar Table 

Calendar = 
            ADDCOLUMNS( 
           CALENDAR("2012,01,01","2021,12,31"),
           "Year", YEAR([Date]),
           "Month Year Num", CONCATENATE(YEAR([Date]), FORMAT([Date],"MMM")),
           "Month Num", Month([Date]),
           "Month", FORMAT([Date], "MMMM"),
           "Quarter Num",FORMAT([Date], "Q"),
           "Quarter", CONCATENATE("Q",FORMAT([Date], "Q")),
           "Day", FORMAT([Date],"D"),
           "FiscalMonth" ,if(MONTH([Date])>=1,MONTH([Date])-0,MONTH([Date])+3),
           "Week", CONCATENATE("Week ",FORMAT([Date], "WW")),
           "FiscalYearNumber",If( Month([Date]) >= 1  , Year([Date]),Year([Date]) -1 
))

Insight:

Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
In 2020, more than 93,000 Americans died from drug overdoses, marking the largest one-year increase in overdose deaths ever recorded, according to provisional data from the U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention. This increase has largely been driven by rising overdoses Men are more than women to use almost all types of illicit drugs, and illicit drug use is more to result in emergency department visits or overdose deaths for men than for women. "Illicit" refers to use of illegal drugs, including marijuana (according to federal law) and misuse of prescription drugs. For most age groups, men have higher rates of use or dependence on illicit drugs and alcohol than do women.14 However, women are just as likely as men to develop a substance use disorder.15 In addition, women may be more susceptible to craving
Step Name:Step 4 Work on Dash Board
Detailed Instructions:

1.make back ground color Gray by click on format page report and then select canvas Background from color select light gray and make Transparence to 0 as Gif below.

2.logo for Colaberry and Connecticut Health Dept you can use photo below and follow me as gif show below.

3. KPIs
A.Total KPI
1. From visuals at right hand select Card visual drag and drop
2. From measure Table chose TotalD Measure 
3. Click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color #E66C37
4. Click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 133
Position horizontal 0 Vertical 93
5. From Title in text box write Total , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
6. From effects ,transparency 100%

B. Age Average
1. Copy total KPI and past then replace total d measure with Age Avg ,
2. Click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text      Size 22 font color Black 
3.Click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 133
   Position horizontal 132 Vertical 93
4. From Title in text box write Age , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
5. From effects ,transparency 100%

C. Male Avg 

1. Copy total KPI and past then replace total d measure with Male Measure
2. Click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text       Size 22 font color #6FA4DB
3. Click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 138
       Position horizontal 265 Vertical 93
4. Title in text box write Male , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
5. From effects ,transparency 100%

D. Female Avg
1. Copy total KPI and past then replace total d measure with Female Avg ,
2. Click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color#9429F0
3. Click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 138
Position horizontal 403 Vertical 93
4. Title in text box write Female , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color         #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
5. From effects ,transparency 100%

E. MoM%
1. Copy total KPI and past then replace total d measure with MoM%
2. Click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color#0EC1E5
3. Click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 162
Position horizontal 542 Vertical 93
4. Title in text box write MoM% , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
5. From effects ,transparency 100%

6. now we need to make arrow that show MoM% is bad or good , in my project case if arrow pointing up is bad that mean red color ,pointing down that mean good green color
7. from chart pan you have to select card and select (arrow M )from measures then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color
8. From  conditional format ,from color callout value ,format style chose field value ,
then in what field should we based this on  chose measure color M

F. YoY%
1. Copy total KPI and past then replace total d measure with YoY% , click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color
#414FB1
2. click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 162
Position horizontal 703 Vertical 93
3. Title in text box write YoY% , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle
4. From effects ,transparency 100%

5. now we need to make arrow that show YoY% is bad or good , in my project case if arrow pointing up is bad that mean red color ,pointing down that mean good green color
6. from chart pan you have to select card and select (arrow Y)from measures then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 22 font color
7. you chose conditional format ,from color callout value ,format style chose field value ,
then in what field should we based this on  chose measure color Y

G. In order to create this first select a "Slicer Visual"
1. from the visualizations tab. then select Year from Calendar Table ,click on format Visual then turn off slicer header, then in values you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 12 font color Black ,background color white ,slider change color to black,
2. click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 282
Position horizontal 865 Vertical 93
3. Title in text box write Year, Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle.

H. Data Refreshed
1. from visuals at right hand select Card visual drag and drop from Date Time from data Refresh Table.
2. click on format Visual then select callout value, you have to change font to Arial ,Text Size 14 font color Black.
3. click now on general and chose properties Size  High 100 ,width 133
Position horizontal 1147 Vertical 93.
4.Title in text box write Data Refresh , Font Arial ,Size 14 ,text color black ,back ground color #E66C37 ,Horizontal alignment chose Middle.
5.effects ,transparency 100%.

Right now we done with all KPIs Just 4 Charts left

portfolioContent.title: Accidental Drug Related Deaths 2012-2021
portfolioContent.portfolioSummary: Developed a Power BI dashboard analyzing accidental drug-related deaths to inform public health interventions and policy decisions.
portfolioContent.summary: This project analyzed drug overdose deaths in Connecticut from 2012 to 2021 using Power BI to visualize trends by demographics and geography. It involved data preparation, DAX calculations, and creating interactive dashboards to highlight key patterns in overdose incidents. The insights support targeted public health strategies to address the opioid crisis.
portfolioContent.businessProblem: The United States experienced a record increase in drug overdose deaths in 2020, driven by illicit drug use with significant gender and age disparities. Public health agencies require detailed analytics to understand these trends and allocate resources effectively. There is a need to identify high-risk groups and regions to reduce overdose fatalities.
objectives: Import and prepare drug overdose data for analysis
Create interactive visualizations to reveal demographic and geographic trends
Provide actionable insights to support public health decision-making
workflow: Import raw overdose data into Power BI
Prepare and clean data including indexing and merging location fields
Create DAX measures for key metrics like total deaths
Design and format interactive dashboards with maps and charts
Analyze trends by year, age, gender, and location
keyInsights: Men have higher rates of illicit drug use and overdose deaths than women across most age groups
Overdose deaths increased sharply in 2020, marking the largest one-year rise recorded
Certain geographic areas show higher concentrations of accidental drug-related deaths
Women are equally likely as men to develop substance use disorders despite lower usage rates
businessImpact: Enabled targeted resource allocation to high-risk demographics and regions
Supported evidence-based public health policies to combat overdose deaths
Improved stakeholder understanding of drug overdose trends through clear visualizations
